opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My foot feels like it is on fire, and even the bedsheet touching it sends me into absolute agony. It's the right foot and lower leg — it's blown up, gone all shiny and blotchy, and tonight the pain has just exploded. I broke that leg a few weeks ago but this is a hundred times worse than the break ever was.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain went from a dull ache to unbearable burning over the last day and I can't put any weight on it or even have the sheet touch it — I'm stuck in bed and I live on my own.

symptom_narrative.onset: the underlying problem started a few weeks ago after I broke my leg, but tonight it suddenly got dramatically worse over about 24 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: weeks of it building, then a big flare in the last day
symptom_narrative.character: burning, like it's on fire, with throbbing when the leg hangs down
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: honestly the worst pain I've had — way out of proportion to the original break, I can't cope with it
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: my right foot and lower leg
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant now, and much worse if the leg is hanging down or anything touches it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: keeping the leg still and stopping anything from touching it — even that barely helps
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the lightest touch, the bedsheet, letting the leg hang down, trying to put on a sock or stand
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: the leg is swollen and the skin looks shiny and tight with red blotchy patches, and it seems to sweat differently to the other leg
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before — it all started after the fracture
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm bedbound tonight, I can't weight-bear or wear a shoe, and I'm worried about falling because I live alone

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: broke a bone in my lower right leg a few weeks ago (a minor fracture)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; worried about falls tonight because the bedroom is cluttered and it's hard to get to the bathroom on one leg

objective_data_offered.value: my right lower leg and foot look swollen, and the skin is shiny and tight with red and blotchy patches over it
the foot is still its normal pinkish colour and feels warm to me, not pale or cold, and I can feel it normally — it's not numb
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-observation, not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: 36.9 on my home thermometer — I don't feel feverish or shivery
the bad leg seems to sweat more/differently than the other one
